name: Home Assistant + Matter Bridge
description: Exposes WiFi-CSI sensing as Home Assistant entities over MQTT auto-discovery, with mDNS announcement on _ruview-ha._tcp and tamper-evident Ed25519-signed audit logs. Adds 10 semantic primitives (someone_sleeping, possible_distress, fall_risk_elevated, ...) on top of the 11 raw measurements. Privacy mode strips biometrics at the wire so only the semantic layer reaches HA — the right default for any deployment with non-tenant occupants.

config.label: Sensing server URL
config.description: Where the cog reads VitalsSnapshot from

config.label: MQTT broker host
config.description: External mosquitto / HA Core MQTT host (v0.7 will add an embedded broker option)

config.label: Privacy mode
config.description: Strip biometrics at the wire — only semantic primitives are published. Recommended for any deployment with non-tenant occupants (care homes, education, shared housing).

config.label: mDNS hostname
config.description: Must end with .local. per RFC 6762. HA's discovery integration looks up this hostname.

config.label: Advertised IPv4
config.description: LAN-routable address the mDNS responder advertises. HA reaches back to this for MQTT.

config.label: Disable mDNS
config.description: Skip the mDNS responder. Useful in containerised setups where multicast is filtered.
